title: The Deadband — Where Change Becomes Imperceptible
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: Every computational system has a deadband — a threshold below which changes are indistinguishable from noise — and this deadband is quantifiable
content.proof: For floating-point arithmetic: the deadband is the ULP (unit in the last place) of the representation. For f64 near 1.0, ULP ≈ 2.2e-16. For snap quantization: the deadband is the difference between adjacent lattice points minus floating-point error. For constraint systems: the deadband is the tolerance below which constraint satisfaction is 'close enough' for practical purposes.
content.benchmark: f64 deadband at 1.0: 2.22e-16. Snap deadband (Eisenstein): 1.5e-15. Constraint satisfaction deadband: configurable, default 1e-12.
content.caveat: Deadband is context-dependent. What's imperceptible in one system is catastrophic drift in another. The art is knowing which context you're in.
